source: Archontakis Barakakis et al. 2023, Int J Chron Obstruct Pulmon Dis, PMC10086393

extraction_ground_truth.design: Prospective, randomized, parallel-group trial across three tertiary medical centers
extraction_ground_truth.dose: Fluticasone 1000 mcg/day + salmeterol 100 mcg/day (high-dose) vs fluticasone 500 mcg/day + salmeterol 100 mcg/day (medium-dose)
extraction_ground_truth.primary_efficacy_outcome: FEV1 improvement and pneumonia incidence over 1 year

key_quotes: 237 COPD patients were randomized into the two treatment arms (115 in the HD group, 122 in the MD group)
The duration of the study period was 1 year
source_evidence.notes: n_active = high-dose (HD) ICS arm (115 randomized to fluticasone 1000 mcg). n_placebo holds the medium-dose comparator (122 randomized to fluticasone 500 mcg). Same convention used throughout r05.

extraction_ground_truth.design: Multicenter, double-blind, placebo-controlled, parallel-group, 52-week phase 3 trial (26-week treatment + 26-week safety extension)
extraction_ground_truth.dose: Mometasone furoate/formoterol (MF/F) 400/10 mcg twice daily (high-dose) vs MF/F 200/10 mcg twice daily (medium-dose); other arms: MF 400 monotherapy, formoterol 10, placebo
extraction_ground_truth.primary_efficacy_outcome: Mean change from baseline in AUC0-12h FEV1 (MF/F vs MF) and AM pre-dose trough FEV1 (MF/F vs F) at week 13

key_quotes: Subjects (n = 1196), at least 40 years old, were current or ex-smokers randomized to twice-daily inhaled MF/F 400/10 μg, MF/F 200/10 μg, MF 400 μg, F 10 μg, or placebo
26-week treatment period and a 26-week safety extension
source_evidence.notes: 5-arm 1:1:1:1:1 randomization of 1196 → ~239 per arm. Per Archontakis high-vs-medium framing, n_active = MF/F 400/10 (high-dose ICS, ~239) and n_placebo = MF/F 200/10 (medium-dose comparator, ~239). The MF 400 monotherapy, formoterol monotherapy, and placebo arms are not represented in n_active/n_placebo per the dose-comparison framing.

extraction_ground_truth.design: Two replicate double-blind, parallel-group, randomized, 1-year trials
extraction_ground_truth.dose: Fluticasone furoate/vilanterol (FF/VI) 200/25 mcg once daily (high-dose) vs FF/VI 100/25 mcg once daily (medium-dose); other arms: FF/VI 50/25 and vilanterol 25 alone
extraction_ground_truth.primary_efficacy_outcome: Yearly rate of moderate and severe exacerbations

key_quotes: 1622 patients in study 1 and 1633 patients in study 2 were randomly assigned
patients were aged 40 years or older
Patients were randomly assigned (1:1:1:1) … to 25 μg vilanterol alone or 25 μg vilanterol combined with either 50 μg, 100 μg, or 200 μg fluticasone furoate once daily
two replicate double-blind parallel-group 1 year trials
source_evidence.notes: Combined two replicate trials: 1622 + 1633 = 3255 total. 1:1:1:1 across 4 arms → ~814 per arm. Per Archontakis high-vs-medium framing, n_active = FF/VI 200/25 (high-dose ICS, ~814) and n_placebo = FF/VI 100/25 (medium-dose comparator, ~814). The FF/VI 50/25 and VI-alone arms are not represented.

extraction_ground_truth.design: Double-blind, parallel-group, multicentre, phase 3 randomised controlled trial
extraction_ground_truth.dose: Budesonide/glycopyrrolate/formoterol (BGF) MDI 320/18/9.6 mcg twice daily (triple therapy with high-dose ICS); comparator: open-label budesonide/formoterol DPI 400/12 mcg (alternative ICS formulation/delivery); other arms: GFF MDI (no ICS) and BFF MDI 320/9.6 (no LAMA)
extraction_ground_truth.primary_efficacy_outcome: FEV1 AUC0-4h and morning pre-dose trough FEV1 over 24 weeks

key_quotes: 1902 were randomly assigned to receive BGF MDI (n=640), GFF MDI (n=627), BFF MDI (n=316), or BUD/FORM DPI (n=319)
Eligible patients were 40-80 years of age
source_evidence.notes: 4-arm 2:2:1:1 randomization. Per Archontakis high-vs-medium ICS dose framing, n_active = BGF MDI 320 mcg budesonide (640) and n_placebo holds the BUD/FORM DPI 400 mcg open-label comparator (319) — these are the two budesonide-containing arms with different ICS doses. The other two arms (GFF MDI no-ICS, BFF MDI no-LAMA at the same 320 mcg budesonide) are not represented in n_active/n_placebo. KRONOS is the most ambiguous fit for the high-vs-medium framing in this set; the chosen comparison is the cleanest dose-difference within the trial.

extraction_ground_truth.design: Phase 3, randomised, double-blind, parallel-group, 12-52 week variable-length study
extraction_ground_truth.dose: Budesonide/formoterol (BFF) MDI 320/10 mcg twice daily (high-dose) vs BFF MDI 160/10 mcg twice daily (medium-dose); third arm: formoterol 10 mcg alone (no ICS)
extraction_ground_truth.primary_efficacy_outcome: Change from baseline in morning pre-dose trough FEV1 at week 12

key_quotes: patients received twice-daily BFF MDI 320/10 µg or 160/10 µg, or FF MDI 10 µg
1843 patients (BFF MDI 320/10 µg, n=619; BFF MDI 160/10 µg, n=617; and FF MDI, n=607)
moderate-to-very severe COPD
source_evidence.notes: 3-arm trial. n_active = BFF 320/10 high-dose (619); n_placebo = BFF 160/10 medium-dose comparator (617). FF MDI (n=607, no ICS) is the third arm, not represented in n_active/n_placebo. Age criterion ≥40 typical for adult COPD trials; not in abstract directly.

extraction_ground_truth.design: Multicenter, randomised, 24-week, double-blind, placebo-controlled, parallel-group study
extraction_ground_truth.dose: FF/VI 200/25 mcg once daily (high-dose) vs FF/VI 100/25 mcg once daily (medium-dose); other arms: FF 200, FF 100, VI 25, placebo

key_quotes: Multicentre, randomised, 24-week, double-blind, placebo-controlled, parallel-group study in stable, moderate-to-severe COPD subjects (N = 1224)
Subjects were randomised to FF/VI (200/25 μg; 100/25 μg), FF (200 μg; 100 μg), VI 25 μg, or placebo, once daily in the morning
source_evidence.notes: 6-arm randomization of 1224 → ~204 per arm. n_active = FF/VI 200/25 (high-dose ICS); n_placebo = FF/VI 100/25 (medium-dose ICS). The four other arms (FF monotherapy 200 and 100, VI 25, placebo) are not represented per Archontakis dose-comparison framing.

extraction_ground_truth.dose: Triple therapy with budesonide 320/18/9.6 mcg (high-dose ICS triple) vs budesonide 160/18/9.6 mcg (medium-dose ICS triple); other arms: glycopyrrolate-formoterol (no ICS) and budesonide-formoterol 320 (no LAMA)
extraction_ground_truth.primary_efficacy_outcome: Annual rate of moderate or severe COPD exacerbations

key_quotes: patients in a 1:1:1:1 ratio … (inhaled glucocorticoid [320 μg or 160 μg of budesonide], a LAMA [18 μg of glycopyrrolate], and a LABA [9.6 μg of formoterol]) or one of two dual therapies
modified intention-to-treat population comprised 8509 patients
1.08 in the 320-μg-budesonide triple-therapy group (2137 patients), 1.07 in the 160-μg-budesonide triple-therapy group (2121 patients)
source_evidence.notes: 4-arm 1:1:1:1 randomization of 8509 patients. Per Archontakis high-vs-medium ICS dose framing, n_active = 320 mcg budesonide triple (2137); n_placebo = 160 mcg budesonide triple (2121). The two non-triple arms (GFF dual no-ICS, BFF dual no-LAMA) are not represented. ETHOS is the largest trial in r05's set.

extraction_ground_truth.design: Randomized, double-blind, double-dummy, parallel-group, 12-month multicenter study
extraction_ground_truth.dose: Budesonide/formoterol pMDI 320/9 mcg twice daily (high-dose) vs BFF pMDI 160/9 mcg twice daily (medium-dose); third arm: formoterol DPI 9 mcg (no ICS)
extraction_ground_truth.primary_efficacy_outcome: COPD exacerbation rate (number per patient-treatment year)

key_quotes: 1219 patients were randomized 1:1:1 to twice-daily budesonide/formoterol pMDI 320/9 μg, budesonide/formoterol pMDI 160/9 μg, or formoterol dry powder inhaler 9 μg
COPD patients aged ≥40 years
source_evidence.notes: 3-arm 1:1:1 randomization of 1219 → ~406 per arm. n_active = BFF 320/9 (high-dose ICS); n_placebo = BFF 160/9 (medium-dose ICS comparator). Formoterol-alone arm (no ICS, ~406) is not represented per Archontakis framing. Age ≥40 explicit in abstract.

extraction_ground_truth.design: 6-month, randomized, double-blind, double-dummy, placebo-controlled, parallel-group, multicentre study
extraction_ground_truth.country_region: International (US, Czech Republic, Netherlands, Poland, South Africa)
extraction_ground_truth.dose: Budesonide/formoterol pMDI 320/9 mcg twice daily (high-dose) vs BFF pMDI 160/9 mcg twice daily (medium-dose); other arms: BUD+FORM monotherapy combined, BUD alone, formoterol alone, placebo

key_quotes: 1704 patients aged > or =40 years with moderate to very severe COPD
budesonide/formoterol pMDI 160/4.5 microg x two inhalations (320/9 microg); budesonide/formoterol pMDI 80/4.5 microg x two inhalations (160/9 microg); ... or placebo
source_evidence.notes: 6-arm randomization of 1704 → ~284 per arm. n_active = BFF pMDI 320/9 (high-dose ICS); n_placebo = BFF pMDI 160/9 (medium-dose ICS comparator). The 4 other arms (combined-monotherapy, BUD alone, formoterol alone, placebo) not represented. Age ≥40 explicit in abstract.

extraction_ground_truth.design: Multicenter, double-blind, placebo-controlled, parallel-group, 52-week phase 3 trial (26-week treatment + 26-week safety extension)
extraction_ground_truth.dose: Mometasone furoate/formoterol (MF/F) 400/10 mcg twice daily (high-dose) vs MF/F 200/10 mcg twice daily (medium-dose); other arms: MF 400, formoterol 10, placebo
extraction_ground_truth.primary_efficacy_outcome: FEV1 AUC0-12 (MF/F vs MF) and morning pre-dose FEV1 (MF/F vs F) at endpoint

key_quotes: Subjects (n = 1055; ≥40 years) were current or ex- smokers randomized to twice-daily treatment with inhaled MF/F 400/10 μg, MF/F 200/10 μg, MF 400 μg, F 10 μg, or placebo
26-week treatment period and a 26-week safety extension
source_evidence.notes: 5-arm randomization of 1055 → ~211 per arm. n_active = MF/F 400/10 (high-dose ICS, ~211); n_placebo = MF/F 200/10 (medium-dose ICS, ~211). MF monotherapy, formoterol, and placebo arms not represented per dose-comparison framing. Same-design replicate of Doherty 2012; both included in Archontakis 2023.

extraction_ground_truth.design: Randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, parallel-group, 24-week multicenter study in Asian COPD patients
extraction_ground_truth.dose: FF/VI 200/25 mcg once daily (high-dose) vs FF/VI 100/25 mcg once daily (medium-dose); other arms: FF/VI 50/25 and placebo
extraction_ground_truth.primary_efficacy_outcome: Change from baseline in trough FEV1 at week 24

key_quotes: 643 patients
randomized (1:1:1:1) to placebo, FF/VI 50/25 mcg, 100/25 mcg or 200/25 mcg once daily
source_evidence.notes: 4-arm 1:1:1:1 randomization of 643 → ~161 per arm. n_active = FF/VI 200/25 (high-dose ICS); n_placebo = FF/VI 100/25 (medium-dose ICS comparator). FF/VI 50/25 and placebo arms not represented per dose-comparison framing. Age ≥40 typical for COPD; not in abstract directly.
